status: DEV — NOT patient-facing; NOT an AU dose source; NOT prescribing guidance. US/EU APPROVED LABEL doses sourced from AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC), stored as what they are: foreign-jurisdiction dose guidance, labelled by jurisdiction. Structurally ISOLATED from the PharmCheck engine (no accessor reads it) so a foreign label can never become an AU dose — the 'Australian healthcare context only' hard limit is preserved by construction, not by wording. Records author to review_status 'draft'.
isolation_note: No PharmDataSource exposes a getInternationalDoseGuidance() accessor and the engine never reads this file. Its only consumers are (a) a clinician reading a foreign label beside the AU dose at the point of decision, and (b) DoseGuidanceSchema.au_congruence, which APPRAISES the AU record against it. The appraisal annotates; it never vetoes an AU dose — an AU dose derives its authority from APF22 / the TGA PI, and letting a foreign regulator veto it would invert the jurisdiction rule.
integrity_bar: A record ships only if it carries a real AMASS RegulatoryCore id (amass_id) for a specific authorisation, plus the approved INDICATION the label dose applies to — because a differing indication is the usual, legitimate reason an AU dose differs from a US/EU one. Label FACTS + citation only: FDA SPL is US public domain and EMA SmPC is © EMA, so no SPL/SmPC prose is reproduced here (the same facts-and-citation basis that governs apf22).

attestation.statement: US/EU label dose guidance — reference only, engine-isolated, NEVER an AU dose. Clinical sign-off is NOT required for this register to be USEFUL (it is foreign regulatory fact, not Breath-Ezy clinical judgement), but it is required before any record is surfaced to a clinician as decision-support context. Empty skeleton — no records authored yet (C1 populates it from AMASS).
attestation.scope: pharm-international-dose-guidance dev v0.1.0 (skeleton — no records authored yet)
